description:
Letter fragment in Judaeo-Arabic, with the address in Arabic script on verso. Fragments of the first ~7 lines are preserved. Dating: probably 12th or 13th century. The addressee is a certain Abū Naṣr Elʿazar who is given some elevated titles (sayyidnā al-sayyid al-ajall). None of the content of the letter is preserved. The address on verso says "to Fustat, the house of al-Ḥakīm al-Raʾīs Abū Naṣr al-Bardādī may God establish his fortune." The name البردادي is very unusual; it might be an error for al-Baghdādī. 

Not digitized.
description_he: שבר מכתב ביהודית-ערבית, עם כתובת בכתב ערבי בגב. הנמען: אבו נצר אלעזר. הכתובת: לפוסטאט, בית אל-חכים אל-ראיס אבו נצר אל-ברדאדי
